skill.name: flowio
skill.description: Parse Flow Cytometry Standard files, inspect metadata and channels, extract event data, and export FCS data for preprocessing workflows.
skill.summary: Handle FCS flow cytometry files for parsing, metadata review, array extraction, and basic export tasks.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: The static external command, script, and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline API names, and FCS channel keywords. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious execution intent was found in SKILL.md or references/api_reference.md. The remaining risk is limited to expected local file parsing and export behavior.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences, inline identifiers, and normal Python examples. They do not instruct the agent to run shell commands beyond a standard package installation example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown examples and API documentation. No shell interpolation, command construction, or user-controlled execution path is present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are FCS Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match FCS terminology such as PnN, PnS, PnR, and PnE, plus the skill description. These are flow cytometry metadata fields, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context defines FCS channel labels and amplification metadata. I did not find hashing, encryption, signature verification, or password storage logic.

low_findings.title: Local File Read and Export Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill teaches reading FCS files and writing FCS or CSV outputs. This is expected for the skill, but users should process trusted files and choose controlled output paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are core to FCS handling and are not malicious. The residual risk is ordinary local file processing risk for malformed inputs or unintended output paths.

content.value_statement: Flow cytometry teams need reliable access to FCS event data and metadata before analysis. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through FlowIO workflows for parsing, inspecting, converting, and exporting FCS files.
actual_capabilities: Read FCS 2.0, 3.0, and 3.1 files with FlowData.
Extract event data as NumPy arrays with optional preprocessing.
Inspect TEXT metadata, channel labels, event counts, and instrument fields.
Handle multi-dataset FCS files with read_multiple_data_sets.
Create or re-export FCS files from array data and channel metadata.
Convert FCS event data into pandas DataFrames or CSV files.
limitations: Does not provide advanced gating, compensation, or FlowJo workspace support.
Requires the FlowIO Python package and compatible Python environment.
Large event files can require substantial memory when fully loaded.
Parsing relaxed offset errors can hide malformed or nonstandard FCS structure.

use_cases.title: Inspect New Cytometry Files
use_cases.description: Review FCS version, event count, channel labels, and acquisition metadata before adding files to a pipeline.

use_cases.title: Prepare Data for Analysis
use_cases.description: Load event matrices into NumPy or pandas for quality checks, transformations, and downstream modeling.

use_cases.title: Build Backend File Workflows
use_cases.description: Create services that validate uploaded FCS files, extract summaries, and export normalized files.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect One FCS File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use FlowIO to inspect this FCS file. Report the FCS version, event count, channel count, channel labels, and key TEXT metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Events to CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me a FlowIO workflow that reads an FCS file, converts events to a pandas DataFrame, preserves channel names, and exports a CSV.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Multi-Dataset Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a robust FlowIO approach for an FCS file that may contain multiple datasets. Include error handling and per-dataset summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Preprocessing Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a FlowIO-based preprocessing pipeline for many FCS files. Include metadata-only scanning, validation, array extraction, filtering, export, and memory safeguards.

output_examples.input: Inspect sample.fcs for basic file and channel information.
output_examples.output: A concise report listing the FCS version, total events, channel count, channel labels, instrument metadata, and acquisition date.

output_examples.input: Convert one FCS file to a tabular format for analysis.
output_examples.output: A workflow that loads event data, assigns channel names as columns, attaches selected metadata, and exports a CSV file.

output_examples.input: Process a file that may contain multiple datasets.
output_examples.output: A plan that detects multi-dataset structure, reads each dataset separately, and summarizes event counts and channel labels.

best_practices: Use metadata-only parsing when event data is not needed.
Validate channel counts and labels before exporting derived files.
Preserve original TEXT metadata when modifying and re-exporting FCS data.
anti_patterns: Do not use relaxed offset parsing without recording that the file was malformed.
Do not load very large event matrices into memory when only metadata is required.
Do not treat FlowIO as a replacement for specialized gating or compensation tools.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Flow Cytometry Standard files, especially FCS versions 2.0, 3.0, and 3.1.

faq.question: Can it perform gating or compensation?
faq.answer: No. It is for file handling and preprocessing. Use FlowKit or another analysis tool for gating and compensation.

faq.question: Can it export data to CSV?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide conversion from FlowIO event arrays into pandas DataFrames and CSV files.

faq.question: Does it support multi-dataset FCS files?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents read_multiple_data_sets for files containing more than one dataset.

faq.question: What Python dependencies are expected?
faq.answer: The workflows require FlowIO and commonly use NumPy. Some examples also use pandas for tabular export.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The static alerts were reviewed as documentation false positives. The main caution is normal local file handling.
